title: Company Secretary Services
description: Private companies in South Africa aren't legally required to appoint a company secretary — but governance obligations don't disappear just because you're a (Pty) Ltd. CIPC filings have deadlines, board resolutions require proper documentation, statutory registers must be maintained, and shareholders need transparency. OptiHR provides professional company secretary services so your governance is handled correctly, without the cost of a full-time appointment.

legalConsiderations.title: Companies Act
legalConsiderations.subtitle: governance obligations for private companies.
legalConsiderations.body: Even (Pty) Ltd companies must comply with the Companies Act — maintaining registers, filing with CIPC, and documenting board decisions correctly.

legalConsiderations.title: CIPC
legalConsiderations.subtitle: annual returns and director updates.
legalConsiderations.body: All companies must file annual returns and notify CIPC of changes to directors, registered addresses, and share structure within prescribed timeframes.

faq.question: Do private companies need a company secretary?
faq.answer: Private companies are not legally required to appoint a company secretary under the Companies Act, but they still have the same governance obligations as public companies. Someone needs to maintain statutory records, file with CIPC, and document board decisions — doing it wrong has consequences.

faq.question: What happens if we miss a CIPC filing deadline?
faq.answer: Late annual returns attract penalties that increase the longer they remain outstanding. Persistent non-compliance can result in the company being deregistered by CIPC, which can have serious legal and commercial implications.

faq.question: What is a statutory register and why does it matter?
faq.answer: Statutory registers include your share register, directors' register, and records of company secretaries. These must be kept up to date and available for inspection. Failure to maintain them correctly can expose directors to personal liability.

faq.question: Can OptiHR handle company secretary services remotely?
faq.answer: Yes. Our company secretary services are designed to work efficiently for any private company in South Africa, regardless of location. We manage filings, records, and governance documentation on your behalf.

faqSubtitle: Common questions about company secretary services
faqBody: Answers to questions we hear from business owners and directors about CIPC compliance and governance.

sections.text: Company secretaries ensure companies comply with the Companies Act, maintain proper records, support boards with governance, and act as the communication link between directors and shareholders . While the Companies Act only mandates company secretaries for public and state-owned companies, private companies still face the same governance obligations—they just don't need a designated person called "company secretary" .
Why private companies benefit from company secretary services:

Governance obligations don't disappear because you're private — Private companies must still maintain statutory registers, file annual returns with CIPC, record board resolutions, keep shareholder records, and comply with the Companies Act . Someone has to do this work—and doing it wrong has consequences .

CIPC deadlines don't wait—and penalties add up fast — Annual returns, director changes, share transfers, MOI amendments—all require CIPC filings within strict deadlines. Late filings result in penalties, deregistration threats, and compliance complications .

Board resolutions need legal precision — Appointing directors, issuing shares, approving financial statements, declaring dividends, entering major contracts—all require properly drafted board resolutions . Informal decisions or incomplete resolutions create legal risks when disputes arise or transactions are challenged .

Investors and lenders demand proper governance — Private equity investors, venture capitalists, banks, and business partners expect professional governance. Poor record-keeping, missing resolutions, or non-compliant processes undermine credibility and deal flow .

Statutory registers aren't optional — The Companies Act requires companies to maintain registers of directors, shareholders, company secretaries (if appointed), beneficial owners, and company securities . Inspectors, auditors, shareholders, and courts can demand access—and missing or incorrect registers create liability .

Directors need independent advice—not just CFO opinions — Company secretaries provide independent governance advice ensuring directors understand their fiduciary duties, conflicts of interest, and legal obligations under the Companies Act . This independence protects directors from inadvertent breaches .
You don't need a full-time employee — Outsourced company secretary services provide professional governance support at a fraction of the cost of hiring someone full-time .
